paragraph.content: It is the sense of the Congress that the Department of State should, in the course of implementing its reporting responsibilities under section 3282(c) of this title, include a summary of demarches that the United States has issued or received from foreign governments with respect to activities which are of significance from the proliferation standpoint.

paragraph.chapeau: For purposes of this section, the term “demarche” means any official communication by one government to another, by written or oral means, intended by the originating government to express—

subparagraph.content: a concern over a past, present, or possible future action or activity of the recipient government, or of a person within the jurisdiction of that government, contributing to the global spread of unsafeguarded special nuclear material or of nuclear explosive devices;
a request for the recipient government to counter such action or activity; or
both the concern and request described in subparagraphs (A) and (B).

note.heading: Effective Date
note.p: Section effective 60 days after Apr. 30, 1994, see section 831 of Pub. L. 103–236, set out as a note under section 6301 of this title.
